tool_use_result.originalFile:
Turn q until it lines up with a key.

q = {queryAngle} deg

{#each scores as s (s.id)}
{s.id}
{fmt(s.score)}
{/each}

lines: +  // When the query lines up with a key, its label would land right on top
+  // of the key's label. Nudge the query label around the circle so the two
+  // never collide, however close the vectors get.

input.old_string:
<line class="query-line" x1={CX} y1={CY} x2={queryTip.x} y2={queryTip.y} />
      <polygon class="query-arrow" points={arrowPoints(queryAngle)} />
      {#each [labelPoint(queryAngle)] as lbl}
input.new_string:
<line class="query-line" x1={CX} y1={CY} x2={queryTip.x} y2={queryTip.y} />
      <polygon class="query-arrow" points={arrowPoints(queryAngle)} />
      {#each [labelPoint(queryLabelAngle(queryAngle))] as lbl}

tool_use_result.originalFile:
Turn q until it lines up with a key.

q = {queryAngle} deg

{#each scores as s (s.id)}
{s.id}
{fmt(s.score)}
{/each}

lines: <text class="label-text query-label" x={lbl.x} y={lbl.y} text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="middle">

content.text:
This resolves the label collision: the query's "q" label now nudges 22 degrees around the circle whenever the query angle sits within 16 degrees of any key, so the label never lands on top of a key's label, at any state or viewport, while the vectors themselves still visibly overlap to show alignment.

DONE Fixed the q/k1 label collision at aligned-with-k1 by adding a queryLabelAngle nudge function that offsets the query's label position 22 degrees around the circle whenever it falls within 16 degrees of any key label, keeping both labels legible while the vectors themselves still visually align.

result:
